sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content: Systemic therapy serves two roles in anal carcinoma: (1) concurrent chemotherapy with radiation for non-metastatic disease, and (2) palliative systemic therapy for metastatic disease. For localized disease, mitomycin/5-FU or mitomycin/capecitabine with concurrent RT are preferred regimens, with cisplatin/5-FU as a category 2B alternative [ANAL-B 1 of 5]. For metastatic disease, carboplatin/paclitaxel plus the PD-1 inhibitor retifanlimab-dlwr is the preferred first-line regimen based on the phase 3 POD1UM-303/InterAACT2 trial showing improved PFS (9.30 vs 7.39 months; HR = 0.63; P = .0006) [234, ANAL-B 2 of 5]. PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors are preferred options for second-line and subsequent therapy [ANAL-B 2 of 5, MS-21, MS-22].

cards.name: Fluorouracil/Mitomycin + RT (localized)
cards.detail: setting: Localized anal canal cancer — concurrent chemoradiation, drugs: [Fluorouracil: 1000 mg/m2/day, IV continuous infusion, Days 1–4 and 29–32; Mitomycin: 10 mg/m2 (capped at 20 mg), IV bolus, Days 1 and 29], cycle_days: Concurrent with RT; repeat 5-FU and mitomycin in week 5, key_toxicities: Grade 3/4 hematologic events, dermatologic toxicity, GI toxicity [100, 189]

cards.name: Fluorouracil/Mitomycin + RT — Alternative dosing (localized)
cards.detail: setting: Localized anal canal cancer — concurrent chemoradiation, drugs: [Fluorouracil: 1000 mg/m2/day, IV continuous infusion, Days 1–4 and 29–32; Mitomycin: 12 mg/m2 (capped at 20 mg), IV bolus, Day 1 (single dose)], cycle_days: Concurrent with RT; single mitomycin dose on day 1, key_toxicities: Similar to standard dosing [ANAL-B 1 of 5]

cards.name: Capecitabine/Mitomycin + RT (localized)
cards.detail: setting: Localized anal canal cancer — concurrent chemoradiation, drugs: [Capecitabine: 825 mg/m2, PO BID, Days 1–5 and 29–33; Mitomycin: 10 mg/m2 (capped at 20 mg), IV bolus, Days 1 and 29], cycle_days: Concurrent with RT; repeat capecitabine and mitomycin in week 5, key_toxicities: Dermatologic toxicity, GI toxicity, hand-foot syndrome [189, ANAL-B 1 of 5]

cards.name: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel + Retifanlimab-dlwr (metastatic first-line)
cards.detail: setting: Metastatic anal cancer — first-line, drugs: [Carboplatin: AUC 5, IV, Day 1; Paclitaxel: 175 mg/m2, IV, Day 1; Retifanlimab-dlwr: 375 mg (flat dose), IV, Day 1], cycle_days: Every 21 days, key_toxicities: Peripheral neuropathy, myelosuppression, immune-related adverse events [234, MS-20]

cards.name: Carboplatin/Paclitaxel (metastatic second-line)
cards.detail: setting: Metastatic anal cancer — second-line and subsequent (no prior immunotherapy), drugs: [Carboplatin: AUC 5, IV, Day 1; Paclitaxel: 175 mg/m2, IV, Day 1], cycle_days: Every 21 days, key_toxicities: Peripheral neuropathy, myelosuppression [ANAL-B 2 of 5, MS-22]
